terms.meaning: A low-fidelity layout that shows structure before visual polish.
A high-fidelity design that looks close to the final product.
What a screen shows when there's no data yet — often missed in first drafts.
The session where work is critiqued before it's handed off.
Passing a finished design to engineering with the specs they need.
Moving code or data from one system or schema to another.
The rota for responding to production incidents out of hours.
Shortcuts in the codebase that slow future work until repaid.
Peer inspection of a change before it merges.
The planned sequence of what's being built and roughly when.
The boundary of what a piece of work includes and excludes.
A decision that gains one thing at the cost of another.
Research to understand a problem before committing to a solution.
A live view of key metrics for a team or stakeholder.
A stated limitation on what a number can be trusted to mean.
A group tracked together over time, e.g. users who signed up the same month.
